The clearest improvement is to revert the seed placement to the proven half-time location. This is the same complexity (7 constants, 29 nodes) but restores the better fit. The physical justification: seed bypasses the slow primary-nucleation lag phase, which most directly shortens the half-time/onset rather than the elongation rate.

- **Numerical stability:** `x2 ≥ 0` and non-negative `c6` keep the denominator `≥ 1`, so no singularities; unseeded curves (`x2 = 0`) reduce exactly to `c2*x1^c3`.
- **Physical justification:** Seed bypasses primary nucleation and shortens the lag phase (half-time), the canonical seeding effect on nucleation-dependent polymerization sigmoids.
